items.title: SK hynix and TetraMem collaborate on experimental chip to bolster energy efficiency for edge AI devices — memristor-based in-memory SoC research leaves performance questions up in the air
items.description: SK hynix, TetraMem, and the University of Southern California built a memristor-based in-memory computing system-on-chip for AI edge devices, achieving promising energy efficiency, but failed to demonstrate its full potential.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter SK hynix, TetraMem, and researchers from the University of Southern California have developed a memristor-based in-memory computing (IMC) system-on-chip (SoC) for AI edge devices. The device is designed to accelerate neural network inference in lightweight AI models while consuming a fraction of the power that higher-end GPUs or NPUs would. To a large degree, the SoC is a proof-of-concept chip, as its performance would peak at around 2.54 TOPS in a theoretical best-case scenario, which is 16X below Microsoft's Copilot+ requirements.A DWC-optimized IMC architectureMemristor-based in-memory computing (IMC) accelerates neural networks by performing analog computations directly inside memory arrays, which reduces data movement and power consumption. However, depthwise convolution (DWC) — a core operation in lightweight networks such as MobileNet — performs independent per-channel filtering with limited data reuse and therefore maps poorly onto conventional crossbar arrays. To address this limitation, researchers from SK hynix, TetraMem, and USC developed an SoC that features both conventional IMC crossbars and a memristor-based IMC architecture specifically optimized for DWC. (Image credit: SK Hynix)The jointly developed SoC is based on an embedded RISC-V processor that schedules workloads and features 10 neural processing units (NPUs). One NPU out of 10 is dedicated to depthwise convolution, while the remaining nine execute pointwise and dense operations. Nine out of 10 NPU include a 256 × 256 memristor crossbar that performs the analog vector-matrix multiplication (VMM), 256 8-bit DACs that convert digital activations into analog voltages, 256 8-bit ADCs that convert the analog outputs back into digital values, and additional peripheral circuitry for reading, writing, programming, and controlling the crossbar.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The DWC-optimized NPU replaces its conventional array with eight specialized 252 × 28 zig-zag crossbar blocks, but retains DACs and ADCs. SK hynix developed and fabricated the memristor devices and integrated the resistive switching cells on top of the 65 nm CMOS circuitry using its back-end process.That DWC-optimized NPU is the key feature of the whole SoC. To accelerate depthwise convolution, TetraMem replaced the straight selection lines used in conventional 1T1R crossbars with a zig-zag topology. As a result, the NPU contains eight 252 × 28 crossbar blocks whose diagonal selection lines activate 252 memory cells across 28 columns, which enables 28 independent 3 × 3 convolutions to run in parallel while using 100% of the array for weight storage. The remaining nine NPUs retain conventional 1T1R crossbars for 1×1 pointwise and dense layers and preserve the throughput and energy efficiency of traditional in-memory computing. You may like SK hynix unveils 'iHBM' thermal architecture that cools AI memory at the source Researchers build brain-like memory device for AI sensors that may improve energy efficiency Laser-driven spintronic memory device switches 1,000 times faster than DRAM Great efficiency, low performance overallTo demonstrate the architecture, the researchers deployed a customized MobileNetV1Small neural network for the Visual Wake Words benchmark. The network contains approximately 36,000 parameters; all depthwise layers were mapped to the dedicated NPU, and pointwise layers were mapped to the remaining NPUs.Because the memristor-based IMC hardware natively performs unsigned analog vector-matrix multiplication, inputs and weights are quantized to unsigned 8-bit values before execution. Since each memristor device can be programmed with only slightly more than 2 bits of effective precision, the design uses a two-subarray compensation technique that boosts effective weight precision to roughly 4 bits.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsConceptually, the approach is somewhat analogous to Nvidia's NVFP4 philosophy, in that both seek to achieve higher effective precision from low-precision hardware. However, the implementations are fundamentally different: NVFP4 relies on a digital floating-point representation and scaling factors, whereas the memristor SoC improves precision by compensating for analog programming errors using two programmed subarrays.When it comes to accuracy, the SoC achieved an end-to-end inference accuracy of 80.36%, which matches the corresponding 4-bit software model. As for performance, the SoC delivers a peak throughput of 0.254 TOPS per NPU and reaches an energy efficiency of 21.3 TOPS/W at 100 MHz and 11.9 TOPS/W at 400 MHz. According to the authors, this compares favorably with published SRAM-based compute-in-memory accelerators despite being manufactured on an older 65 nm process. The SoC also exceeds Nvidia's A100 INT8 energy efficiency by an order of magnitude, the joint paper claims. Yet, these claims are largely unsubstantiated.First up, the MobileNet demonstration does not even use all 10 NPUs. It uses one dedicated DWC NPU, five standard NPUs for pointwise layers, and leaves four standard NPUs idle. The demonstration thereby does not reveal total SoC throughput (TOPS), sustained throughput running a real network, and throughput with all 10 NPUs simultaneously saturated. In fact, the paper does not even reveal whether all 10 NPUs can be used at the same time. To that end, the 2.54 TOPS figure we mentioned earlier in the story is highly theoretical.Validated approachSK hynix, TetraMem, and researchers from the University of Southern California have developed a memristor-based IMC SoC featuring a novel depthwise convolution accelerator that improves crossbar utilization for lightweight AI workloads. The partners have managed to fabricate it using an outdated 65nm process technology and make it work, achieving a 21.3 TOPS/W energy efficiency and inference accuracy comparable to a 4-bit software model despite the fact that memristors can be programmed with a circa 2-bit accuracy. While the architecture validates that the approach works, the paper does not disclose the full performance of the SoC, and it is not clear whether the chip's 10 NPUs can be saturated at all. items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: SK海力士与TetraMem合作开发实验性芯片，以提升边缘AI设备的能效——基于忆阻器的存内计算SoC研究使性能问题悬而未决
items.descriptionZh:
SK海力士、TetraMem公司与南加州大学的研究人员共同开发了一款基于忆阻器的存内计算（IMC）系统级芯片（SoC），专为AI边缘设备设计。该芯片旨在加速轻量级AI模型中的神经网络推理，功耗仅为高端GPU或NPU的极小部分。从很大程度上说，这款SoC是一个概念验证芯片，其理论最佳性能峰值约为2.54 TOPS，比微软Copilot+的要求低16倍。

该芯片的核心是一种针对深度可分离卷积（DWC）优化的存内计算架构。传统上，基于忆阻器的存内计算通过在存储阵列内部直接执行模拟计算来加速神经网络，从而减少数据移动和功耗。然而，深度可分离卷积——MobileNet等轻量级网络的核心操作——执行独立逐通道滤波，数据复用有限，因此在传统交叉阵列上映射效果不佳。为解决这一限制，联合团队开发的SoC融合了传统IMC交叉阵列和专门针对DWC优化的忆阻器存内计算架构。

该SoC基于嵌入式RISC-V处理器进行任务调度，集成了10个神经处理单元（NPU）。其中1个NPU专用于深度可分离卷积，其余9个执行逐点卷积和密集层操作。9个通用NPU各包含一个256×256的忆阻器交叉阵列，用于模拟向量-矩阵乘法（VMM），以及256个8位DAC（将数字激活转换为模拟电压）、256个8位ADC（将模拟输出转回数字值），以及额外的外围电路用于读写、编程和控制交叉阵列。

而专为DWC优化的NPU则用八个特殊的252×28锯齿形交叉阵列块替换了传统阵列，但保留了DAC和ADC。SK海力士负责开发和制造忆阻器器件，并通过其后端工艺将阻变存储单元集成在65纳米CMOS电路之上。

这个DWC优化NPU是整个SoC的关键。为加速深度可分离卷积，TetraMem将传统1T1R交叉阵列中的直线选择线替换为锯齿形拓扑。由此，该NPU包含八个252×28交叉阵列块，其对角线选择线可激活28列上的252个存储单元，使得28个独立的3×3卷积能够并行运行，同时阵列的100%存储空间都被用于权重存储。其余9个NPU保留传统1T1R交叉阵列，用于1×1逐点卷积和密集层，保持了传统存内计算的吞吐量和能效。

为了验证架构，研究人员部署了一个定制的MobileNetV1Small神经网络用于Visual Wake Words基准测试。该网络包含约36000个参数；所有深度可分离卷积层映射到专用NPU，逐点卷积层映射到其余NPU。由于基于忆阻器的存内计算硬件原生执行无符号模拟向量-矩阵乘法，输入和权重在执行前被量化为无符号8位值。而每个忆阻器器件仅能被编程约2位有效精度，因此设计采用了两子阵列补偿技术，将有效权重精度提升至约4位。概念上，这类似于NVIDIA的NVFP4理念——从低精度硬件中获得更高有效精度——但实现方式根本不同：NVFP4依赖数字浮点表示和缩放因子，而此忆阻器SoC通过使用两个编程子阵列补偿模拟编程误差来提高精度。

在精度方面，该SoC达到了80.36%的端到端推理准确率，与对应的4位软件模型相当。性能方面，单NPU峰值吞吐量为0.254 TOPS，能效在100MHz下为21.3 TOPS/W，在400MHz下为11.9 TOPS/W。论文称，尽管采用较老的65nm工艺制造，这一表现仍优于已发表的基于SRAM的存内计算加速器，并且相比NVIDIA A100 INT8的能效高出一个数量级。然而这些说法很大程度上未经充分证实。

首先，MobileNet演示并未使用全部10个NPU：它使用了1个专用DWC NPU、5个标准NPU用于逐点卷积层，剩余4个标准NPU闲置。因此，演示未揭示SoC的总吞吐量（TOPS）、运行真实网络时的持续吞吐量，以及全部10个NPU同时饱和时的吞吐量。实际上，论文甚至未说明所有10个NPU是否能同时使用。前文提到的2.54 TOPS数值是高度理论化的。

总体而言，SK海力士、TetraMem和南加州大学成功开发了一款基于忆阻器的存内计算SoC，其创新的深度可分离卷积加速器改善了轻量级AI工作负载下的交叉阵列利用率。合作方使用过时的65nm工艺完成了制造并使其工作，达到了21.3 TOPS/W的能效，且尽管忆阻器仅能被编程约2位精度，推理精度仍与4位软件模型相当。该架构验证了这一技术路线的可行性，但论文未披露SoC的全部性能，且尚不清楚芯片的10个NPU能否同时达到饱和状态。

items.title: Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026) Review: Stunning Mini‑LED, serious muscle, and a few missed steps
items.description: The Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 pairs an 18-inch mini-LED display with cutting-edge components, but omissions like PCIe 5.0 storage and dual-channel RAM —plus slightly weaker performance than Razer’s Blade 18 —keep it from taking top honors.
items.content: Asus’ ROG Strix Scar 18 (starting at $4,299.99) is an example of abundance in the world of gaming laptops, built around an 18-inch display and the latest flagship silicon: a Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus with Nvidia’s GeForce RTX 5090 in our test unit. This machine makes a loud first impression, from its stellar (albeit tricky to configure) mini-LED display to the unique scrolling marquee lighting on its lid. But at this price - $4,999.99 as tested – the Scar 18 must prove it can hold the line against Razer’s Blade 18 before it can claim a spot at the top.Design of the Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026)At 15.71 x 11.73 x 1.38 inches, the Scar 18 has the footprint of a cafeteria tray – this isn’t a laptop you’ll be getting out on a plane. And at 8.16 pounds, this is also one of the heaviest laptops on the market. But performance is the goal here, not portability. Razer’s Blade 18 (15.74 x 10.84 x 1.1 inches) is thinner and significantly lighter, at 7.06 pounds.Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026) (1TB 32GB RAM) at Amazon for $4,299.99Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The vibrant Aura Sync lightbar around the base of the laptop also demands attention, as does the RGB-lit Republic of Gamers logo on the lid. Both are configurable with customizable lighting and patterns in the Armoury Crate app.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The lid also has a special feature called AniMe vision, a diagonally scrolling marquee of text via LEDs shining through holes in the lid backing. (This is extremely similar to the AniMe Matrix that debuted on the Zephyrus line years ago.) There are several preconfigured versions of the Republic of Gamers logo, and you can add your own text effects. Layered effects are possible and don’t always produce the desired effect — I had a “raining” effect enabled at the same time as my text, and the text was almost impossible to make out.The bottom line is that the Scar 18 couldn’t do anything more to look like a gaming laptop – it is designed to be seen. Build-wise, it’s a solid machine, showing minimal flex no matter how I handled it. Only the lid is metal, with the rest of the construction thick plastic.Connectivity is thoroughly modern: two Thunderbolt 5 (USB-C) and three USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, HDMI 2.1, an audio combo jack, and 2.5 Gbps Ethernet. Internally, it offers Wi-Fi 7 and Bluetooth 5.4 from an Intel BE200 networking card. The power connector is proprietary for the 450 W power brick.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)SpecificationsSwipe to scroll horizontallyCPUIntel Core Ultra 9 290HX PlusGraphicsNvidia GeForce RTX 5090 (24GB GDDR7, 1,597 MHz boost clock, 175 W maximum graphics power)Memory32GB DDR5-6400 (1x 32GB)Storage1TB PCIe 4.0 SSD (HFS001TEJ9X101N)Display18-inch, 3840 x 2400, 16:10, Mini-LED, G-Sync, 240 Hz, anti-glareNetworkingIntel Wi-Fi 7 BE200, Bluetooth 5.4Ports2x Thunderbolt 5, 3x USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-A, HDMI 2.1, 3.5 mm combo audio jack, 2.5 Gbps EthernetCameraFHD IRBattery90 WHrPower Adapter450 W (proprietary connector)Operating SystemWindows 11 HomeDimensions (WxDxH)15.71 x 11.73 x 1.38 inches (39.9 x 29.8 x 3.5 cm)Weight8.16 pounds (3.7 kg)Price (as configured)$4,999.99Today's best Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026) deals $4,299.99Preorder $5,224.34View No price informationCheck Best BuyWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricesGaming and Graphics on the Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026)We tested the ROG Strix Scar 18 in flagship form, featuring a Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus processor, RTX 5090 graphics card (175 W maximum graphics power), and 32GB of RAM. This is top-of-the-line gaming technology, though with one misstep: single-channel RAM. This might affect its performance as we’re about to see. The Task Manager confirms that only one SO-DIMM slot was used.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)I put the Scar 18 through its paces playing 007: First Light at 3840 x 2400 with all detail settings maxed out. At first, this proved too demanding – I saw 26 to 32 frames per second (FPS) in most scenes. Enabling DLSS more than doubled the frame rate – I saw around 70 FPS or better, and the game was supremely playable.Our comparison lineup includes Alienware’s 16 Area-51 (RTX 5080), MSI’s Raider 16 Max HX (RTX 5090), and Razer’s Blade 18 (RTX 5090). All laptops use a Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus and have GPUs rated for 175 W like our Asus. Their native screen resolutions, however, are different: Alienware and MSI are 2560 x 1600 while Razer has a unique dual-model display supporting both 1920 x 1200 and 3840 x 2400.Image 1 of 5(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The Scar 18 was competitive across the board at 1200p, typically a few FPS ahead of the Alienware but a few FPS behind the MSI and particularly the Razer.Bumping the resolution to 4K, the Scar 18 trailed the Razer in most games – the delta was at or almost 10% in Shadow of the Tomb Raider, Far Cry 6, Cyberpunk 2077, and F1 23. (Red Dead Redemption 2 was the exception.) While those numbers won't make the difference between playability and unplayability, the price of these laptops makes it difficult to overlook.Differences versus the Razer aside, the Scar 18 still demonstrates ample performance for gaming at 4K in most of the games we tested, though not all – it averaged only 21 FPS in Cyberpunk 2077 on ray tracing ultra, indicating that it won't be possible to play every game at maximum detail settings.We stress test gaming laptops running 15 loops of the Metro Exodus stress test at RTX settings. During the test, the Scar 18 averaged 141 FPS with minimal variance between runs, starting at 141.5 FPS and finishing at 141.1 FPS. The Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus CPU averaged 4.59 GHz on its P-cores and 2.58 GHz on its E-cores while the RTX 5090 had an average boost clock of 1.98 GHz.Productivity Performance on the Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026)We tested the Scar 18 with a Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus CPU, 32GB of RAM, and a 1TB PCIe 4.0 SSD. Not including a PCIe 5.0 drive seems like a missed opportunity at this price, though Razer does the same thing.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)In Geekbench 6, the single-core numbers between these laptops were almost indistinguishable as we might expect given they all use the Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus, though the Scar 18’s multi-core score of 17,629 points significantly trailed the others, which all scored over 20,000 points.The Scar 18 landed middle of the road in our 25GB file transfer test, averaging 2,042.69 MBps, putting it ahead of the MSI (1,357.93 MBps) and Razer (1,670.53 MBps) but behind the Alienware (2,738.9 MBps).The Scar 18 also proved competitive in our 4K-to-1080p Handbrake transcoding test, completing it in two minutes and 11 seconds to lead the Alienware (2:24) but trail the MSI (1:51) and Razer (2:01).Display on the Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026)The Scar 18’s “Nebula” HDR display is its crowning feature. With a 3840 x 2400 (4K) resolution, mini-LED backlighting with 2,000 dimming zones, a 240 Hz refresh rate, and Nvidia G-Sync, this is quite advanced.Tweaking is required to get this display to perform as intended, and it’s not simple. Out of the box, HDR is disabled, the refresh rate is capped at 120 Hz, and G-Sync is unavailable since Nvidia Optimus, which dynamically switches between the CPU’s integrated graphics and the RTX 5090, is enabled. To unlock maximum performance, the GPU must be put in “Ultimate” mode in Armoury Crate, which is effectively a MUX toggle that disables the integrated graphics. A restart is required for this to take effect. G-Sync, the 240 Hz refresh rate, and a special feature called “Extreme Low Motion Blur” (ELMB) then become available. The latter is aimed at esports players– it manipulates the pixels so that they turn off when switching colors, theoretically eliminating blur. (More on this in a moment.)Those aren’t the only settings you’ll need to know about. You can toggle the mini-LED backlighting control between one zone, multi-zone balanced, or multi-zone strong. These settings produce very different images – one-zone provides the deepest contrast, multi-zone balanced is the dimmest but evens out the contrast to make dark scenes appear brighter, and multi-zone strong is the brightest and most vibrant. I stuck with the latter for nearly everything.ELMB only works in one-zone mode without HDR. I tested it using the Blur Blusters UFO Test. It clearly made a difference – in the 240 fps scrollbar, the UFO looked crisp moving across the screen. Disabling ELMB caused it to become blurry, making it harder to see details. This feature can really matter for competitive esports.But wait, there are even more settings! Armoury Crate includes many color modes through a feature called GameVisual — racing, scenery, RTS/RPG, fps, cinema, eyecare, vivid, and e-reading. On top of that, it also provides color temperature and gamut settings.Then there’s the question of HDR. To get that working, it must first be enabled in the Windows Settings app. Back in Armoury Crate, you’ll find GameVisual, color temperature and gamut settings, Extreme Low Motion Blur, and mini-LED backlighting settings are no longer available. That’s the trade-off.Complicating all this is that the settings I just mentioned are in different places in Armoury Crate. Some are in the display section, while others require going into the Devices section, selecting the Scar 18, and making changes there. It’s not straightforward, and those that simply use this laptop out of the box without tweaking won’t get the best visual experience.After much experimentation, I played 007: First Light in GPU Ultimate mode, G-Sync enabled, a 240 Hz refresh rate, and True Color HDR enabled through Windows. The picture left little to the imagination – colors seemed to pop off the screen and the 4K resolution provided exquisite detail right down to the patches on Bond’s uniform. HDR effects from muzzle flashes and explosions were dazzling in dark environments.When I watched Zootopia 2, I switched off HDR and used SDR multi-zone strong. Colors looked exquisite, and brightness was borderline excessive in a darker room. Bright objects like lamps almost seemed overexposed, but they weren’t – the display was simply that bright, and colors were so saturated that I found it hard to look away. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The Scar 18’s numbers are from its default out-of-the-box display settings. In color coverage, its 77.9% DCI-P3 coverage was last in the group – Alienware's OLED screen achieved 93.7% — but is still high enough to create vibrant-looking colors. Its 428.2-nit peak brightness was mid-pack, brighter than the Alienware's 368.6 nits but well back from Razer's 538 nits.Also shown in our charts are the multi-zone strong settings, which produced 584.6 nits of brightness, with parts peaking at 625 nits. Enabling HDR, we measured an astounding 1,124 nits at 10%, 1,090 nits at 40%, and 943 nits at 100%. If you're looking for one of the brightest laptop displays around, the Scar 18 ranks high on the list.Keyboard and Touchpad on the Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026)The Scar 18’s keyboard is great for gaming – the keys require enough actuation force that resting your fingers on WASD or the arrow keys won’t produce accidental presses. Key travel is communicative in the sense your fingers know exactly when a key is at the top or bottom of the stroke. The bright RGB backlight is sharp and easy to see. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The keyboard is less ideal for productivity. The tactile feel is rather lifeless, though I still managed 126 words per minute with 99% accuracy in my usual MonkeyType run. Layout-wise, a two-thirds-size number pad on an 18-inch laptop is a miss – there’s plenty of space to make it full-size. Additionally, the arrow key cluster isn’t separated out, resulting in no right Ctrl key, and there are no dedicated Home, End, Page Up, or Page Down keys. Asus does, however, provide five dedicated macro keys, a rarity on any laptop. These are configurable in the Armoury Crate app.Asus’ mechanical touchpad is excellent, with an expansive surface and a smooth but fingerprint-resistant surface coating. Its clicking action is quiet.Audio on the Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026)The Scar 18’s quad-speaker array delivers a decent, if not remarkable, audio experience. In 007: First Light, soft details like the footsteps of approaching enemies were easy to pinpoint, thanks to the expansive soundstage – there’s plenty of room to separate the speaker placement on a laptop this large. Bass is muted, though, resulting in explosions and gunfire that don’t stir up as much excitement as they could.In Phil Collins’ “Don’t Lose My Number,” high hats on drum hits were sharp but missed low-end bump. Switching to the Chainsmokers’ “Summertime Friends,” I also noted the lack of bass, though the vocals were crisp. The overall sound signature is on the hollow side, but that can be sharpened up using the Atmos Detailed equalizer in the Dolby Access app. None of the equalizers made up for the lack of bass, though. Volume levels are also moderate – I found myself pushing at least 80% volume for most situations.Upgradeability of the Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026)Getting inside the Scar 18 couldn’t be easier – simply slide the latch below the palm rest, slide the entire bottom panel forward, and lift it away. You don't even need tools.Upgrade possibilities include two M.2 slots, two SODIMM slots, and the battery.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Battery Life on the Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026)Our battery test consists of web browsing, running OpenGL tests, and streaming videos with the screen at 150 nits while connected to Wi-Fi. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)One minute shy of the five-hour mark, the Scar 18 demonstrates respectable battery life for an 18-inch gaming laptop. The Razer lasted half an hour longer (5:31) and the MSI Raider (8:34) clearly does a better job conserving power, but the Scar 18 did outperform the Alienware (3:33) by several hours.Heat on the Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026)We measure the surface temperatures of gaming laptops while running the Metro Exodus stress test. Peak temperatures were 91 degrees Fahrenheit on the keyboard between the G and H keys, 90 F on the touchpad, and 108 F on the underside near the cooling vents. Internally, the Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus averaged 66 Celsius while the RTX 5090 ran at 64 C.The laptop’s fans are well-behaved for daily use. Though fan noise increases while gaming, I had no trouble hearing footsteps and distant conversations in 007: First Light using the built-in speakers.Webcam on the Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026)Asus’ FHD webcam has the minimum resolution expected on a modern laptop. The picture looks soft and washed out. Highlights aren’t handled that well – a window in the background appeared blown out – and I had trouble making out details on my face from just a few feet away. Gamers who value visual quality will want to invest in an external webcam.Software and Warranty on the Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026)Asus includes a useful software bundle, starting with the familiar Armoury Crate. This app provides component monitoring, a macro editor, game library, an exhaustive amount of display settings, and lighting settings via Aura Sync and AniMe Vision. Accessing some settings is unintuitive since you need to go to the Device section and select the laptop. There you can access Windows key and Touchpad toggles and several display settings, including panel overdrive (240 Hz refresh rate). Most settings can be saved in profiles.The MyAsus app is more generic. In addition to diagnostics and system updates, it provides a battery care mode, microphone noise cancelation, and networking preferences that allow prioritizing traffic to games or other apps.†The Scar 18 also works with Asus’ GlideX app to share content across devices, including phones and tablets.Asus includes a standard one-year warranty.ConfigurationsAsus offers two Scar 18 configurations with only the GPU different between them – model G835LWG-DB96 uses an RTX 5080 for $4,299.99 while our review model, G835LXG-DB96, steps up to the RTX 5090 for $4,999.99. All other components are the same: a Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus processor, 32GB of RAM, a 1TB SSD, and the 18-inch mini-LED display.Pricing is slightly higher than Razer’s Blade 18 with the RTX 5080 – it was $4,099 at this writing. Razer runs $5,399 with the RTX 5090, but that price also includes a 2TB SSD.Bottom LineThe Scar 18 is an undeniably impressive machine that goes all-in on visuals. Its mini-LED “Nebula” display looks breathtaking when properly configured, producing exceptional brightness. The AniMe scrolling marquee, dedicated macro keys, and easy serviceability also elevate its appeal.However, when it comes to performance, the Scar 18’s single-channel RAM and lack of a PCIe 4.0 SSD are significant shortcomings on a $4,999.99 machine. Several of our gaming benchmarks and multi-core CPU performance showed meaningful dips against Razer’s Blade 18. Additionally, while its display is brilliant, the maze of settings required to unlock its potential means it doesn’t provide the best experience out of the box.Overall, the Scar 18 is a formidable and visually stunning laptop with plenty of power and one of the best displays you’ll find in a laptop. It simply doesn’t perform consistently enough to displace the Blade 18 as our top recommendation among elite 18-inch gaming laptops.Asus ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026): Price Comparison $4,299.99Preorder $4,999.99Preorder $5,224.34View No price informationCheck Best BuyWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by

items.titleZh: 华硕ROG Strix Scar 18 (2026)评测：惊艳的Mini-LED，强劲的性能，以及若干不足之处
items.descriptionZh:
华硕ROG Strix Scar 18（2026款）评测：视觉震撼但性能存憾的顶级游戏本

华硕ROG Strix Scar 18（起售价4299.99美元）是游戏笔记本电脑领域“堆料”的典型代表，围绕一块18英寸显示屏和最新旗舰芯片打造——测试机搭载Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus处理器与Nvidia GeForce RTX 5090显卡。这款机器从第一印象就声势夺人：其mini-LED显示屏效果惊艳（尽管配置过程繁琐），机盖上还有独特的滚动跑马灯文字效果。但在这个价位——测试机型售价4999.99美元——Scar 18必须证明它能与Razer Blade 18正面抗衡，才能跻身顶级行列。

**设计：为性能而生，牺牲便携性**

Scar 18的尺寸为15.71 x 11.73 x 1.38英寸，占地面积如同食堂托盘——显然不适合在飞机上使用。重量达8.16磅（约3.7公斤），是市面上最重的笔记本电脑之一。设计目标是性能而非便携。相比之下，Razer Blade 18（15.74 x 10.84 x 1.1英寸）更薄且显著更轻，仅7.06磅（约3.2公斤）。

机身底部的Aura Sync灯条和机盖上的RGB“玩家国度”logo同样引人注目，两者都可通过Armoury Crate应用自定义灯光和模式。机盖还有一个名为AniMe Vision的特殊功能：LED透过机盖背板上的孔洞显示对角线滚动的跑马灯文字（与多年前Zephyrus系列首发的AniMe Matrix极为相似）。有多种预设的“玩家国度”logo版本，也可添加自定义文字效果。叠加效果有时并不理想——我同时启用了“下雨”效果和文字，结果文字几乎无法辨认。

总而言之，Scar 18在外观上已经做到了游戏本的极致——它就是为引人注目而设计。做工方面，机身坚固，无论我如何用力拧转都几乎没有形变。只有机盖是金属材质，其余部分为厚实塑料。

连接性十分现代：两个Thunderbolt 5（USB-C）、三个USB-A 3.2 Gen 2接口、HDMI 2.1、音频组合插孔以及2.5Gbps以太网。内部采用Intel BE200无线网卡，支持Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙5.4。电源接口为专用接口，配合450W电源适配器。

**规格一览**
- CPU：Intel Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus
- 显卡：Nvidia GeForce RTX 5090（24GB GDDR7，boost频率1597 MHz，最高图形功耗175W）
- 内存：32GB DDR5-6400（单条32GB）
- 存储：1TB PCIe 4.0 SSD
- 显示屏：18英寸，3840×2400分辨率，16:10比例，mini-LED，G-Sync，240Hz刷新率，防眩光
- 网络：Intel Wi-Fi 7 BE200，蓝牙5.4
- 端口：2×Thunderbolt 5，3×USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-A，HDMI 2.1，3.5mm音频组合插孔，2.5Gbps以太网
- 摄像头：FHD红外
- 电池：90Wh
- 电源：450W（专用接口）
- 操作系统：Windows 11 Home
- 尺寸：15.71×11.73×1.38英寸（39.9×29.8×3.5 cm）
- 重量：8.16磅（3.7 kg）
- 测试配置价格：4,999.99美元

**游戏与图形性能：4K游刃有余，但单通道内存拖后腿**

我们测试的旗舰版Scar 18搭载Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus处理器、RTX 5090显卡（最高175W）和32GB内存。这是顶级的游戏技术，但有一个失误：单通道内存（仅用了一个SO-DIMM插槽）。这可能会影响性能。

我以3840×2400分辨率、全部最高细节设置运行《007：第一缕光》。起初过于吃力——大部分场景只有26-32 FPS。开启DLSS后帧率翻倍以上，达到约70 FPS或更高，游戏流畅度极佳。

对比机型包括Alienware 16 Area-51（RTX 5080）、MSI Raider 16 Max HX（RTX 5090）和Razer Blade 18（RTX 5090）。所有机型均使用Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus，显卡额定功率均为175W。但它们原生屏幕分辨率不同：Alienware和MSI为2560×1600，Razer则采用独特的双模显示器，支持1920×1200和3840×2400。

在1200p分辨率下，Scar 18整体表现有竞争力，通常比Alienware高出几帧，但比MSI和特别是Razer低几帧。提升至4K分辨率后，Scar 18在大多数游戏中落后于Razer——《古墓丽影：暗影》、《孤岛惊魂6》、《赛博朋克2077》和《F1 23》中的差距约为或接近10%（《荒野大镖客：救赎2》除外）。虽然这些数字不会影响可玩性，但考虑到这些笔记本电脑的价格，差距难以忽视。

不过，与Razer的差距之外，Scar 18在大部分测试游戏中仍能提供足够的4K游戏性能，但并非全部——在光线追踪超高画质下，《赛博朋克2077》平均仅21 FPS，表明无法以最高画质运行所有游戏。

我们在RTX设置下运行15轮《地铁：离去》压力测试。Scar 18平均帧率为141 FPS，各轮间差异极小（从141.5 FPS到141.1 FPS）。CPU P核平均频率4.59 GHz，E核平均2.58 GHz；RTX 5090平均boost频率1.98 GHz。

**生产力性能：多核表现明显落后**

Scar 18搭载Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus CPU、32GB内存和1TB PCIe 4.0 SSD。在此价位未配备PCIe 5.0硬盘似乎是个遗憾（Razer也是如此）。

在Geekbench 6中，单核分数几乎无差异（毕竟都使用相同的CPU），但Scar 18的多核分数为17,629分，显著落后于其他所有机型（均超过20,000分）。在25GB文件传输测试中，Scar 18以平均2042.69 MB/s居中，领先MSI（1357.93 MB/s）和Razer（1670.53 MB/s），但落后于Alienware（2738.9 MB/s）。在4K转1080p的Handbrake转码测试中，Scar 18用时2分11秒，领先Alienware（2分24秒），但落后于MSI（1分51秒）和Razer（2分01秒）。

**显示屏：惊艳但配置繁琐**

Scar 18的“Nebula”HDR显示屏是它的王牌。3840×2400（4K）分辨率、2000个调光区的mini-LED背光、240Hz刷新率和Nvidia G-Sync，规格相当先进。

但要让显示屏按预期工作需要进行调整，而且并不简单。开箱时，HDR被禁用，刷新率锁定在120Hz，由于启用了Nvidia Optimus（动态切换集显和独显），G-Sync不可用。要解锁最高性能，必须在Armoury Crate中将GPU设为“Ultimate”模式（相当于MUX切换，禁用集显），然后重启。之后，G-Sync、240Hz刷新率以及一项名为“极致低运动模糊”（ELMB）的特殊功能才可用。ELMB专为电竞玩家设计——通过在颜色切换时关闭像素来理论上消除模糊。

你还需要了解其他设置。你可以将mini-LED背光控制切换为单区、多区平衡或多区强。这些设置产生截然不同的图像：单区对比度最深，多区平衡最暗但能平衡对比度使暗场景更亮，多区强则最亮最鲜艳。我几乎一直使用后者。

ELMB仅在单区模式下且无HDR时工作。我用Blur Busters UFO测试进行了验证——在240fps滚动条中，UFO在屏幕上移动时看起来清晰。禁用ELMB后变得模糊，细节更难辨认。这对竞技电竞很有用。

还有更多设置！Armoury Crate通过GameVisual功能提供多种色彩模式——赛车、风景、RTS/RPG、FPS、影院、护眼、鲜艳和阅读。此外，还提供色温和色域设置。

至于HDR，需要先在Windows设置中启用，然后回到Armoury Crate，你会发现GameVisual、色温、色域、ELMB和mini-LED背光设置均不再可用——这就是权衡。

更复杂的是，这些设置分布在Armoury Crate的不同位置。有些在显示部分，有些则需要进入设备部分、选择Scar 18并进行更改。这并不直观，那些不进行调整、直接开箱使用的用户无法获得最佳视觉体验。

经过大量尝试后，我以GPU Ultimate模式、开启G-Sync、240Hz刷新率和Windows True Color HDR游玩《007：第一缕光》。画面细节丰富，色彩仿佛要跳出屏幕，4K分辨率让邦德制服上的补丁都清晰可见。在黑暗环境中，枪口闪光和爆炸的HDR效果令人目眩。

当我观看《疯狂动物城2》时，我关闭HDR使用SDR多区强模式。色彩精致，在较暗的房间亮度几乎过高。像台灯这样的明亮物体几乎过曝，但并非如此——显示器就是那么亮，色彩饱和度高到让人难以移开视线。

Scar 18的测试数据来自默认出厂的显示设置。在色彩覆盖方面，其77.9%的DCI-P3覆盖在对比组中最低（Alienware的OLED屏幕达到93.7%），但仍足以呈现鲜艳色彩。峰值亮度428.2尼特处于中游，高于Alienware的368.6尼特，但远低于Razer的538尼特。

我们测试的多区强模式产生了584.6尼特亮度，部分区域峰值达到625尼特。启用HDR后，我们测得了惊人的数据：10%窗口1124尼特，40%窗口1090尼特，100%窗口943尼特。如果你在寻找最亮的笔记本显示屏之一，Scar 18名列前茅。

**键盘与触控板：游戏优秀，打字平淡**

Scar 18的键盘非常适合游戏——按键需要足够的按压力，使得手指放在WASD或方向键上不会误触。键程反馈明显，手指能清楚知道按键在行程顶部还是底部。明亮的RGB背光清晰易见。

键盘在生产力方面稍逊一筹。触感略显死板，不过我在MonkeyType测试中仍达到了126 WPM、99%的准确率。布局方面，在18英寸笔记本上使用三分之二大小的数字键盘令人遗憾——空间足够做全尺寸。此外，方向键没有独立分区，导致没有右Ctrl键，也没有专用的Home、End、Page Up、Page Down键。不过华硕提供了五个专用宏按键，这在任何笔记本上都属罕见，可在Armoury Crate中配置。

华硕的机械触控板表现出色，表面宽敞且光滑防指纹，点击动作安静。

**音频：声场开阔，低音不足**

Scar 18的四扬声器阵列提供了尚可但不算出色的音频体验。在《007：第一缕光》中，细微的脚步声等细节容易定位，声场开阔——这么大的机身有足够空间安置扬声器。但低音不足，导致爆炸和枪声的冲击力不够。

在Phil Collins的《Don't Lose My Number》中，鼓点的高音清脆但缺乏低频。切换到The Chainsmokers的《Summertime Friends》同样注意到低音缺失，但人声清晰。整体声音偏空，但可使用Dolby Access应用中的Atmos Detailed均衡器进行调整。不过，任何均衡器都无法弥补低音的不足。音量也适中——大多数情况下我需要调到至少80%音量。

**可升级性：非常简便**

无需工具：只需滑动掌托下方的闩锁，将整个底盖向前滑动即可打开。内部有两个M.2插槽、两个SO-DIMM插槽和电池。

**电池续航：中规中矩**

我们的电池测试包括网页浏览、运行OpenGL测试和流媒体播放，屏幕亮度150尼特并通过Wi-Fi连接。Scar 18续航接近5小时（4小时59分），对于18英寸游戏本而言表现尚可。Razer多出半小时（5小时31分），MSI Raider（8小时34分）显然更省电，但Scar 18比Alienware（3小时33分）长几个小时。

**散热：表面温度可接受**

在《地铁：离去》压力测试中，键盘G和H键之间最高温度91华氏度（约33℃），触控板90华氏度（约32℃），底部靠近散热口处108华氏度（约42℃）。内部Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus平均66℃，RTX 5090平均64℃。

日常使用时风扇噪音控制良好。虽然游戏时风扇噪音增加，但我使用内置扬声器时仍能听清脚步声和远处对话。

**摄像头：仅满足最低要求**

华硕的FHD网络摄像头满足现代笔记本的最低分辨率要求。画面偏软、泛白，高光处理不佳（背景窗户过曝），从几英尺外难以看清面部细节。重视画质的玩家最好购置外置摄像头。

**软件与保修：功能丰富但设置分散**

华硕提供了有用的软件包，包括熟悉的Armoury Crate。该应用提供组件监控、宏编辑器、游戏库、海量显示设置以及通过Aura Sync和AniMe Vision的灯光设置。部分设置访问不直观——需要进入设备部分选择笔记本，才能找到Windows键和触控板开关以及包括面板过冲（240Hz）在内的多项显示设置。大多数设置可保存为配置文件。

MyAsus应用更通用，除诊断和系统更新外，还提供电池保养模式、麦克风降噪和网络偏好设置（可优先分配流量给游戏或其他应用）。Scar 18还支持华硕GlideX应用，可在手机和平板等设备间共享内容。保修期为标准一年。

**配置选项**

华硕提供两种Scar 18配置，仅GPU不同：型号G835LWG-DB96使用RTX 5080，售价4299.99美元；我们的评测机型G835LXG-DB96升级至RTX 5090，售价4999.99美元。其他组件相同：Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus处理器、32GB内存、1TB SSD和18英寸mini-LED显示屏。

定价略高于Razer Blade 18的RTX 5080版本（当时售价4099美元）。Razer的RTX 5090版本售价5399美元，但包含2TB SSD。

**总结：视觉王者，性能有憾**

Scar 18无疑是一款令人印象深刻的机器，在视觉方面不遗余力。其mini-LED“Nebula”显示屏在正确配置后效果惊人，亮度非凡。AniMe滚动跑马灯、专用宏按键和简便的可维护性也提升了吸引力。

然而在性能方面，单通道内存和仅配备PCIe 4.0 SSD对一款4999.99美元的机器来说是重大短板。多个游戏基准测试和多核CPU性能均明显落后于Razer Blade 18。此外，虽然显示屏出色，但解锁其潜力需要穿越复杂的设置迷宫，导致开箱即用体验不佳。

总体而言，Scar 18是一款强大且视觉震撼的笔记本电脑，拥有充足的性能和市面上最好的显示屏之一。只是它表现不够稳定，无法取代Blade 18成为精英18英寸游戏本的首选推荐。

items.title: SK hynix raises a record $26.5 billion in historic U.S. IPO — South Korean memory giant to fund massive HBM manufacturing expansions
items.description: SK hynix raised $26.5 billion in a record-breaking Nasdaq IPO, as it plans to channel the windfall from surging AI demand and sold-out HBM supply to fund new fabs.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter SK hynix has completed the largest-ever foreign company IPO in U.S. history, raising $26.5 billion in its Nasdaq debut today, July 10. The South Korean memory giant sold 177.9 million American depositary receipts (ADRs) — a U.S.-listed stand-in for a foreign share — at $149 apiece, each representing a tenth of a Seoul-listed share. The offering was more than seven times oversubscribed and drew demand from more than 500 investment firms, according to Financial Times. Temporary Nasdaq trading is underway under the ticker SKHYV before regular-way trading begins as SKHY on Monday, July 13.The offering was led by Bank of America, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, and JPMorgan, with nine additional firms rounding out a 13-bank syndicate. Anchor demand came from heavyweight institutions including Baillie Gifford, Coatue Management, and Situational Awareness Partners, which together signaled interest in as much as $7 billion of stock, according to people familiar with the matter cited by Financial Times.SK hynix is the world's leading maker of high-bandwidth memory (HBM), the vertically stacked DRAM that has become critical infrastructure for AI accelerators. The company has said it will steer the proceeds toward boosting its AI-memory manufacturing capacity. Confirmed build-outs include the first-phase fab at the massive Yongin semiconductor cluster, a new P&T7 advanced-packaging line in Cheongju, and EUV lithography equipment slated for delivery by the end of next year. Separately, SK hynix is constructing its first U.S. production site, a $4 billion advanced-packaging plant in West Lafayette, Indiana, targeted for completion around 2028. The facility is eligible for up to $458 million in CHIPS Act grants and up to $570 million in federal loans.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: What display resolution do you use on your primary monitor? SK hynix is seeing sensational growth thanks to the ongoing AI boom. The company is reportedly on track to post over 200 trillion won ($133 billion) in operating profit this year, a record-breaking figure that would see SK hynix employees earn around $400,000 each in bonuses. The company’s Seoul-listed stock is up roughly 220% year-to-date and has climbed more than sixfold over the past year.In late June, SK hynix briefly surpassed Samsung as South Korea's most valuable company, closing at around 2,080 trillion won (about $1.35 trillion), a meteoric rise for a company that almost declared bankruptcy in 2001 and, more recently, recorded an annual operating loss of 7.73 trillion won in 2023. That rise doesn't seem like it will be slowing down any time soon. SK hynix has said its entire 2026 output of HBM, DRAM, and NAND is already sold out, with the crunch expected to extend into 2027. items.titleZh: SK海力士进行历史性美国IPO，筹集创纪录的265亿美元——韩国存储巨头为大规模HBM制造扩张提供资金
items.descriptionZh:
韩国存储巨头SK海力士于7月10日在纳斯达克成功上市，完成美国历史上规模最大的外国公司首次公开募股（IPO），募资总额达265亿美元。此次IPO共发行1.779亿股美国存托凭证（ADR），每股定价149美元，每份ADR代表十分之一股在韩国上市的普通股。据《金融时报》报道，此次发行获得超过7倍的超额认购，吸引逾500家投资机构参与。在7月13日（周一）正式以SKHY代码开始常规交易之前，SK海力士的ADR已在纳斯达克以SKHYV代码进行临时交易。

本次上市由美国银行、花旗集团、高盛和摩根大通联合牵头，另有9家机构组成共计13家银行的承销团。根据知情人士向《金融时报》透露的信息，基石投资者包括Baillie Gifford、Coatue Management和Situational Awareness Partners等重量级机构，这三家合计表达了高达70亿美元的认购意向。

SK海力士是全球领先的高带宽内存（HBM）制造商。HBM是一种垂直堆叠的DRAM芯片，通过硅通孔（TSV）技术实现层间互联，相比传统DRAM能够提供更大的带宽、更低的功耗和更小的物理占用空间，已成为AI加速器不可或缺的基础设施。随着大语言模型和生成式AI对算力需求的急剧膨胀，HBM作为GPU内存系统的核心部件，在AI训练和推理过程中承担着数据传输的关键角色。

公司明确表示，IPO募集资金将主要用于扩充AI内存制造产能。已确认的扩张计划包括：位于龙仁大规模半导体集群的第一阶段晶圆厂建设；在清州新建先进的P&T7封装产线；以及计划于明年底前交付的极紫外（EUV）光刻设备。此外，SK海力士正在美国印第安纳州西拉斐特建设其首个美国生产基地——一座耗资40亿美元的先进封装工厂，预计2028年左右完工。该工厂有资格获得最高4.58亿美元的《芯片法案》拨款和最高5.7亿美元的联邦贷款。

受益于持续的人工智能热潮，SK海力士业绩呈现爆炸式增长。据报道，该公司今年有望实现超过200万亿韩元（约1330亿美元）的营业利润，创下历史纪录，届时每位员工有望获得约40万美元的奖金。截至7月，SK海力士在韩国上市的股票今年累计上涨约220%，过去一年涨幅超过六倍。今年6月下旬，该公司市值一度超越三星电子，成为韩国市值最高的公司，收盘市值约2080万亿韩元（约1.35万亿美元）。对一家曾在2001年濒临破产、且在2023年录得7.73万亿韩元年度亏损的企业而言，这无疑是惊人的逆转。

SK海力士的增长势头短期内看不到放缓迹象。公司表示，其2026年全年的HBM、DRAM和NAND闪存产能已经全部售罄，供应紧张局面预计将延续至2027年。在当前全球AI芯片供不应求、各大科技巨头竞相扩充算力基础设施的背景下，SK海力士作为核心供应商将继续扮演关键角色，其IPO的落地也为公司下一阶段的技术升级和产能扩张注入了强大资本动力。

items.title: Would you host part of an AI data center in your home?
items.description:
A solar and home energy storage company is expanding into AI data centers, but not by building one - instead, it's offering to pay its customers to put its compute units in their homes. Sunrun is launching a pilot program for a new "distributed AI compute" program that will "place numerous compute nodes in homes equipped with Sunrun solar and battery storage systems." Customers will be "compensated" for participating in the pilot program. 
Sunrun plans to sell the distributed compute power from the nodes to "enterprise compute buyers," like AI companies. It's a new approach to finding resources and space for AI compute infrastructure, as da …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: A solar and home energy storage company is expanding into AI data centers, but not by building one — instead, it’s offering to pay its customers to put its compute units in their homes. Sunrun is launching a pilot program for a new “distributed AI compute” program that will “place numerous compute nodes in homes equipped with Sunrun solar and battery storage systems.” Customers will be “compensated” for participating in the pilot program.Sunrun plans to sell the distributed compute power from the nodes to “enterprise compute buyers,” like AI companies. It’s a new approach to finding resources and space for AI compute infrastructure, as data centers face increasing pushback across the country. A survey released in May showed that over 70 percent of Americans oppose construction of new data centers in their area, often because of concerns over pollution, noise, and water and electricity use.Rather than consolidating computing power into one large data center, Sunrun’s program would spread it out in smaller compute units dotted all over the country. Sunrun says it previously ran a “successful” proof of concept for this program, but it’s not yet clear how well it will work. This is a totally new area for Sunrun, which mainly focuses on home energy storage. Sunrun’s 1.1 million customers can sign up for the pilot program waitlist if they’re open to hosting a compute node. The company says it “expects to complete the pilot over the coming months and will assess results” before rolling out the program more widely.
items.author: Stevie Bonifield
items.titleZh: 你愿意在家中托管AI数据中心的一部分吗？
items.descriptionZh:
Sunrun（太阳跑）是一家专注于家用太阳能和储能系统的公司，最近宣布启动一项名为“分布式AI计算”的试点项目，但它的方式并非建设传统数据中心，而是将计算设备直接安装到客户家中。根据计划，Sunrun将在那些已经安装了其太阳能板和电池储能系统的家庭中放置“多个计算节点”，参与的客户将因此获得补偿。Sunrun则将这些节点产生的分布式算力打包出售给“企业算力买家”，例如人工智能公司。

这一创新模式的出现，背景是美国各地对新建数据中心的反对声浪日益高涨。今年5月发布的一项调查显示，超过70%的美国人反对在自己所在区域建设新的数据中心，主要担忧包括污染、噪音以及对水电资源的大量消耗。传统数据中心需要巨大的电力供应和冷却系统，往往引发社区不满，且审批周期漫长。Sunrun的分布式方案试图绕开这一困境：它不将计算资源集中到一个巨型数据中心，而是将其分散到全国各地的家庭中，每个家庭只需容纳一个小型计算单元。

从技术原理上看，Sunrun的分布式算力节点将依托家庭已有的太阳能发电和储能系统供电。这意味着计算节点可以利用白天太阳能板产生的电力，并在夜间或阴天时由储能电池维持运行，从而降低对电网的额外负担。这种安排还使算力部署更加灵活——家庭用户不需要专门的服务器机房，只需提供一个能放置计算设备的空间（如车库或地下室），同时节点本身的散热和噪音问题也需要做针对性优化。Sunrun此前已经完成了一个“成功”的概念验证，证明该模式在技术上是可行的，但尚未公布验证的具体数据或性能指标。

关于性能参数和对比数据，新闻中并未给出具体的节点算力、功耗、延迟等数字，也没有与大型数据中心单位算力成本的直接对比。但可以推断，分布式计算的单节点算力必然远小于大型数据中心的集中式方案，其优势在于地理分散带来的供电灵活性、较低的土地和建设成本，以及更易获得社区许可。缺点则包括节点管理复杂、网络延迟可能较高（尤其对于需要低延迟的AI推理任务），以及家庭环境对设备可靠性的影响。

该技术对行业的影响在于，它可能为AI算力基础设施建设提供一条全新的路径。随着AI模型训练和推理对算力的需求爆发式增长（例如OpenAI、谷歌等公司的千亿参数模型），传统数据中心扩张面临越来越大的环境和社会阻力。分布式算力可以充分利用现有住宅屋顶的太阳能资源，减少对电网的依赖，同时将算力部署到电力充足或电价较低的地区。对Sunrun而言，这是一个全新的业务领域——公司此前主要业务是家用能源存储，客户规模约110万家庭。通过这一试点，Sunrun既能增加收入来源，也能为AI公司提供一种更“绿色”的算力选择。

目前该试点项目处于招募阶段，Sunrun的客户如果愿意在家中托管一个计算节点，可以报名加入等待名单。公司表示“预计在未来几个月内完成试点，并根据结果评估效果”，之后再决定是否大规模推广。应用场景方面，分布式算力更适合对延迟要求不高的AI推理任务（如批量数据处理、模型微调、边缘智能），而不太适合需要实时响应的大规模训练任务。不过，如果试点成功，这一模式有望成为AI基础设施去中心化趋势中的一个重要案例。

items.title: Researchers turn HBM on its side to tackle AI memory’s heat wall — Korean V-Die and Japanese MOSAIC designs promise higher bandwidth, denser stacks, and cooler future GPUs
items.description: Researchers in Korea and Japan have proposed sideways-stacked DRAM designs that could push future AI memory beyond conventional HBM limits by improving cooling, bandwidth, and capacity while reducing reliance on TSV-heavy vertical stacks.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Researchers in Korea and Japan have presented two separate memory-integration proposals that aim to increase HBM (High-Bandwidth Memory) capacity and bandwidth without trapping more heat inside ever-taller DRAM (Dynamic Random Access Memory) stacks, one of the most pressing challenges facing future AI accelerators. Presented at the 2026 IEEE/JSAP Symposium on VLSI Technology and Circuits held in June, the two approaches — V-Die from a Korean research collaboration and MOSAIC from a University of Tokyo-led group — both explore the same broad idea of standing DRAM memory dies on their edges instead of stacking the memory dies only upward like conventional HBM.The Korean proposal, called Vertical-Die (V-Die), was presented by researchers at the Ulsan National Institute of Science and Technology (UNIST). The design rotates custom DRAM dies upright, drops through-silicon vias to free die area for more memory cells, gives each die its own bottom-edge I/O, and runs liquid-cooling channels between adjacent dies. In simulations against an HBM4 system at equal capacity, the V-Die system reportedly achieved 540 tokens per second on a GPT-3-sized workload, compared to 296 tokens per second for HBM4.Go deeper with TH Premium: Memory (Image credit: SK Hynix)AI data centers are swallowing the world's memory and storage supplyThe future of DRAM: From DDR5 to future ICsHigh-bandwidth memory roadmapHere's why HBM is coming for your PC's RAMThe Japanese project, MOSAIC, takes a similar “sideways stack” idea but focuses on the practical difficulty of connecting so many vertical dies to a GPU or package substrate. Presented by University of Tokyo researchers, the MOSAIC work uses orthogonal die stacking and a contactless die-to-die interface, in which data is transferred through tiny inductive coils rather than requiring every signal pad to land perfectly on a physical contact. The researchers say the prototype interface achieved up to 4 Gbps per channel, while the memory structure could double HBM4-class capacity in a DRAM-on-GPU configuration.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Both projects aim to solve the growing problem of AI chips being held back by memory. Modern accelerators can perform enormous amounts of computation, but large, powerful models depend on moving huge amounts of data between memory and compute. This is why HBM has become one of the defining technologies of modern AI hardware.The technology addresses the memory wall by stacking multiple DRAM dies vertically on a base die and placing that stack very close to the processor. Nvidia's Blackwell Ultra B300, for instance, carries up to 288GB of HBM3E memory, without which much of the silicon would sit idle waiting for data. The dies are connected via through-silicon vias (TSVs) — tiny vertical channels etched through the silicon and filled with metal. You may like SK hynix unveils 'iHBM' thermal architecture that cools AI memory at the source JEDEC releases new SPHBM4 standard to slash AI memory costs Samsung and SK hynix warn AI-driven memory shortages could last until 2027 and beyond, as HBM demand explodes The stack then communicates with the GPU over an extremely wide interface, often routed through a silicon interposer or an advanced package. This is the core reason HBM can deliver terabytes per second of bandwidth: it uses a very wide, very short data path instead of sending memory traffic across a motherboard, as with conventional DIMMs (Dual In-line Memory Modules), physical sticks of RAM used in computers.However, that same structure creates several problems. While taller stacks add more capacity, they also make it harder to remove heat. Heat generated in the lower dies and at the high-speed interface must pass through layers of silicon, bonding materials, underfill, and package structures before it reaches a heat spreader. Furthermore, TSVs consume die area that could otherwise be used for memory cells, and as bandwidth rises, more routing and I/O place additional pressure on both signal integrity and packaging costs.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsHBM4, the latest generation of HBM, addresses a number of these challenges. Meanwhile, companies such as SK hynix, Samsung, and Micron are racing to improve speed, capacity, base-die performance, and thermal management. SK hynix has already shown iHBM, which embeds cooling elements into the HBM interface area, and Samsung has shown an HBM5 mockup with Heat Path Block cooling to more directly extract heat from the stack. However, they all retain the same upward stacking structure.This convention is what V-Die and MOSAIC are challenging. By standing DRAM dies upright, the researchers expose far more silicon surface area to the cooling path. In theory, this turns the memory stack into something closer to a heat-sink fin array, where heat can move laterally and escape more directly instead of being trapped in the middle of a thick vertical pile. It also opens the door to new connection schemes along the bottom or side of each die, rather than forcing every die to communicate through TSVs running vertically through the stack.For V-Die, the key shift is removing TSVs from the memory dies and replacing them with bottom-edge connections. Each DRAM die gets its own I/O along the bottom edge and connects directly to the substrate, with links reportedly spaced every 20 microns. The team says this layout gives four times as many connections as HBM4 and cuts memory read time by 37%, although some signals must travel farther across the package to reach the processor. What to read next Samsung shows first HBM5 mockup with Heat Path Block cooling Qualcomm reveals HBC near-memory AI architecture, AI250 and AI350 accelerators SoftBank subsidiary working with Intel to develop radical new ZAM memory is now receiving Japanese gov't subsidies Cooling is the other half of the V-Die argument. The proposal places microfluidic cooling channels between adjacent upright DRAM dies, allowing coolant to dissipate heat closer to its source. According to the researchers, this could keep the stack around 45°C, far below the 80°C-plus range associated with dense HBM systems. In a simulated 16-die stack matched to H100-class hardware on a GPT-3-scale model, V-Die hit 540 tokens per second, compared to HBM4's 296, and cut first-token latency by 32%, or about 24 milliseconds.MOSAIC, meanwhile, is focused on making the sideways stack manufacturable. Because the dies are assembled flat and then turned on edge, even a few microns of die-thickness variation across dozens of dies can add up to an alignment miss where the signal pads no longer land. The Japanese team’s answer is a contactless interface based on inductive coupling. One side of the memory die carries oblong coils, while a corresponding set of coils sits on the substrate or mating chip. Current in one coil induces a signal in the other, allowing data to cross the small gap without a direct metal-to-metal signal contact. This eliminates the need for precise overlapping, giving the package greater tolerance for assembly variation. Power, which requires fewer, larger connections than data, can still be supplied via physical contacts on the sides of the memory cube.The VLSI MOSAIC prototype achieved up to 4 Gbps per channel and demonstrated TSV-free 3D integration for a memory-on-GPU layout. The team says the approach can enable twice the memory capacity of HBM4 without significantly increasing peak temperature. A related bump-MOSAIC hardware demonstration at ECTC used 100-micron-pitch microbumps, achieved stacking alignment within 6 microns as verified by X-ray CT, and showed a configuration with three times the thermal conductivity of conventional stacking while adding up to 30% more memory capacity.While the results look promising, neither V-Die nor MOSAIC is close to replacing commercial HBM. Neither is close to shipping. V-Die is still a proposed architecture, with a prototype in the works to validate its thermal and electrical behavior; MOSAIC has proof-of-principle hardware, but the researchers have yet to show it scales to commercial DRAM capacity, yield, cost, and reliability.Still, any viable solution to the multifaceted AI memory problem is a welcome development. SoftBank and Intel’s Z-Angle Memory (ZAM) and NEO Semiconductor’s 3D X-DRAM — both still in development — aim to solve the constraints of conventional memory. Meanwhile, the overall market is already feeling the squeeze on price and availability, even as memory makers divert capacity toward the more lucrative AI HBM and server products, driving consumer RAM prices even higher. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Etiido Uko
items.titleZh: 研究人员将HBM侧向放置以突破AI存储器的热障——韩国V-Die和日本MOSAIC设计承诺更高带宽、更密集堆叠和更冷却的未来GPU
items.descriptionZh:
韩国和日本的研究团队在2026年IEEE/JSAP VLSI技术与电路研讨会上分别提出了两种全新的内存集成方案，旨在突破高带宽内存（HBM）在容量和带宽上的瓶颈，同时解决因DRAM堆叠层数增加而导致的热量积聚难题——这是未来AI加速器面临的最严峻挑战之一。这两个方案分别是韩国研究机构的“V-Die”和东京大学主导的“MOSAIC”。它们共享一个核心设计理念：将DRAM芯片竖立放置（即“侧向堆叠”），而不是像传统HBM那样仅仅向上堆叠。

韩国的V-Die方案由蔚山科学技术院（UNIST）的研究人员提出。其设计将定制的DRAM芯片旋转90度竖直放置，并通过在芯片底部边缘引入垂直通孔（TSV）来释放原本被TSV占用的芯片面积，从而容纳更多存储单元。每个芯片都拥有独立的底部边缘I/O接口。更为关键的是，V-Die在相邻的竖直芯片之间引入微流体冷却通道，使冷却液能更贴近热源。在针对HBM4同容量系统的仿真测试中，V-Die在运行GPT-3规模工作负载时，每秒可处理540个token，而HBM4仅为296个token。此外，V-Die的首个token延迟降低了32%（约24毫秒），芯片堆叠温度可维持在45°C左右，远低于传统HBM系统常见的80°C以上高温。

日本的MOSAIC项目也采用了类似的“侧向堆叠”思路，但重点解决了连接多个竖直芯片的制造难题。由于芯片在组装时是平放而后被竖立，即使每个芯片厚度仅有几微米的差异，叠加后也可能导致信号焊盘无法精确对齐。东京大学团队的解决方案是采用正交芯片堆叠和无接触式芯片间接口（基于电感耦合）。每个存储芯片上带有椭圆形线圈，基板或配对芯片上则有对应的线圈组。通过电流在一个线圈中感应出另一个线圈的信号，数据无需物理金属触点即可跨越微小间隙传输。这大大降低了组装精度的要求，而电源（需要更少但更大的连接）仍可通过芯片侧面的物理触点供给。MOSAIC原型在VLSI展示中实现了每通道4 Gbps的传输速率，并验证了无TSV的3D集成技术。在DRAM-on-GPU配置下，该架构可将HBM4级别的容量翻倍。在ECTC会议上，一项相关的bump-MOSAIC硬件演示采用了100微米间距的微凸点，经X射线CT验证，堆叠对准精度在6微米以内，其热导率是传统堆叠的三倍，同时可增加最多30%的内存容量。

这两项工作直接回应了AI芯片面临的内存墙问题。现代AI加速器计算能力极强，但大型模型依赖海量数据在内存和计算单元间高速移动，HBM正是为此而生。传统HBM通过垂直堆叠DRAM芯片并利用硅通孔（TSV）连接，再通过极宽的接口（如硅中介层）与GPU通信，从而实现TB/s级带宽。然而，随着堆叠层数增加，热量难以从中间层导出；TSV本身占用宝贵的芯片面积；更高的带宽也增加了信号完整性和封装成本的压力。尽管HBM4、SK海力士的iHBM（在接口区嵌入冷却元件）、三星的HBM5 Heat Path Block冷却方案等都在努力改进，但它们都沿用了向上堆叠的结构。

V-Die和MOSAIC从根本上挑战了这一惯例。通过竖立芯片，它们暴露了更大的硅表面积用于散热，理论上将内存堆叠转变为类似散热鳍片的阵列，使热量能横向传导并更直接地逸出。V-Die去掉了芯片上的TSV，采用20微米间距的底部边缘连接，连接数是HBM4的四倍，同时将内存读取时间缩短37%。MOSAIC则通过无接触电感耦合解决了竖立芯片的对准难题，避免了物理焊盘精确重合的要求，从而简化制造。

尽管这些结果令人鼓舞，但无论是V-Die还是MOSAIC都远未达到替代商用HBM的程度。V-Die目前仍处于架构设计阶段，原型正在开发中，以验证其热学和电气特性；MOSAIC虽验证了原理硬件，但尚未展示其能否在商用DRAM的容量、良率、成本和可靠性方面达到要求。不过，任何可行的AI内存问题解决方案都备受期待。软银与英特尔合作的Z-Angle Memory（ZAM）、NEO Semiconductor的3D X-DRAM等也在开发中，旨在解决传统内存的局限。与此同时，由于内存制造商将产能转向利润更高的AI HBM和服务器产品，整个市场已感受到价格和供应紧张的压力，消费者RAM价格因此进一步上涨。

items.title: Micron lifts U.S. spending to $250 billion — company takes $500 million position in America's only 300 mm wafer plant
items.description: Micron has said it will invest up to $3 billion in the US semiconductor supply chain, with $500 million of that going to GlobalWafers.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Micron has said it will invest up to $3 billion in the U.S. semiconductor supply chain, with $500 million of that going to GlobalWafers as strategic financing for its 300 mm raw silicon wafer plant in Sherman, Texas, alongside a 10-year agreement giving Micron access to that plant's wafer output. In a separate announcement, the memory maker raised its planned U.S. spending to more than $250 billion through 2035, up from $200 billion, and confirmed the first concrete pour at its Clay, New York campus more than a quarter ahead of schedule.Sherman is the sole operating facility in the U.S. capable of producing advanced 300 mm raw silicon wafers, the substrate on which every leading-edge DRAM, NAND, and logic die is built. GlobalWafers opened the plant in May last year and holds a $406 million CHIPS Act award covering the site and a silicon-on-insulator facility in St. Peters, Missouri. The 142-acre campus is designed for up to six phases, one of which is running. Micron's other American sites draw their wafers from Japan, Taiwan, Germany, and South Korea; Shin-Etsu, SUMCO, GlobalWafers, Siltronic, and SK Siltron together control the overwhelming majority of global 300 mm supply, making raw silicon the most concentrated layer in the chip space.Tom's Hardware Premium Roadmaps (Image credit: Future)High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) Roadmap Nvidia Enterprise GPU and CPU RoadmapAI accelerator RoadmapDesktop GPU Roadmap3D NAND RoadmapDoris Hsu, chairperson and CEO of GlobalWafers, set out her terms for building phase two at Sherman during the plant's opening last year, telling Reuters the company needed profitability at the first two phases, customers willing to sign long-term contracts, reasonable pricing, prepayments, and government support. Thursday's announcement supplies most of that list in a single transaction.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Wafer suppliers spent the 2023-2024 downcycle protecting margins rather than adding capacity, and SUMCO is winding down 200mm production at Miyazaki this year while holding the line on new 300mm expansion. Customers, not suppliers, are therefore now underwriting the capacity. The last time the industry did this, during the 2017-2018 megacycle, chipmakers signed prepaid long-term agreements that turned into liabilities when pricing rolled over.Silicon wafer shipments reached 3,275 million square inches in Q1 2026, up 13.1% year over year, with SEMI.org attributing the growth to AI data center demand across advanced logic, memory, and power devices. Micron's first new Idaho fab, ID1, is expected to begin wafer output in mid-2027, and production at Clay isn't expected until around 2030. The company began making 1-alpha DRAM at its Manassas, Virginia fab in May. You may like Micron's Virginia fab begins producing America's most advanced DRAM memory Nanya to quadruple capital spending to $6.2 billion in 2027 as DRAM prices push gross margin to 79.5% Nvidia and Intel tout homegrown American chip supply chain prowess as country bolsters local production, but gaps remain Micron told investors last December that it can serve only half to two-thirds of customer demand, and nothing announced Thursday changes the supply position of DRAM this year or next. items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 美光将美国支出提高至2500亿美元——该公司在美国唯一300毫米晶圆厂投资5亿美元
items.descriptionZh:
美国存储芯片巨头美光科技（Micron）宣布将向美国半导体供应链投资高达30亿美元，其中5亿美元将作为战略融资注入全球晶圆供应商GlobalWafers，用于其在德克萨斯州谢尔曼（Sherman）的300毫米（mm）原材料硅片工厂。同时，双方签署了一份为期十年的供应协议，确保美光能够获取该工厂的硅片产出。在另一份声明中，美光将其截至2035年的美国本土支出计划从原先的2000亿美元上调至超过2500亿美元，并确认其位于纽约州克莱（Clay）园区的首期混凝土浇筑已提前超过一个季度完成。

谢尔曼工厂是目前美国唯一能够生产先进300毫米原材料硅片的运营设施——这种硅片是每一块先进DRAM、NAND和逻辑芯片的基底。GlobalWafers于去年5月正式投产该工厂，并已获得《芯片与科学法案》（CHIPS Act）4.06亿美元的拨款，涵盖该工厂以及位于密苏里州圣彼得斯（St. Peters）的绝缘体上硅（SOI）设施。这座占地142英亩的园区设计可进行多达六期扩建，目前一期已投入运营。美光其他美国工厂的硅片供应主要来自日本、台湾、德国和韩国；全球300毫米硅片市场由信越化学（Shin-Etsu）、SUMCO、GlobalWafers、Siltronic和SK Siltron五家厂商牢牢把控，这使得原材料硅片成为芯片制造领域集中度最高的环节。

GlobalWafers董事长兼首席执行官徐秀兰（Doris Hsu）在去年工厂开业时曾明确列出二期建设的条件：一期和二期必须实现盈利、客户愿意签署长期合同、合理的定价、预付款以及政府支持。周四公布的这笔交易几乎一次性满足了上述所有条件。在2023至2024年的下行周期中，硅片供应商选择保护利润率而非增加产能；例如SUMCO今年正逐步关闭其宫崎（Miyazaki）的200毫米生产线，同时对新300毫米扩产按兵不动。因此，如今的实际产能扩张由客户而非供应商来承担风险——上一次行业出现这种模式是2017至2018年的超级周期，当时芯片制造商签署的预付长期协议在随后的价格回调中变为负债。

硅片出货量数据印证了需求回暖：2026年第一季度（请留意原文时间为“Q1 2026”，可能为笔误或未来预期数据，实际报道时间应为近期），全球硅片出货量达到32.75亿平方英寸，同比增长13.1%，SEMI组织将此归因于人工智能数据中心对先进逻辑、存储和功率器件的强劲需求。美光在爱达荷州的首座新工厂（ID1）预计于2027年年中开始产出晶圆，而克莱工厂的投产时间大约在2030年。今年5月，美光已在弗吉尼亚州马纳萨斯（Manassas）工厂开始生产1-alpha制程DRAM。美光在去年12月对投资者表示，其产能仅能满足客户需求的50%至三分之二；周四宣布的所有投资都不会改变当前及未来一年DRAM的供应状况。

items.title: Samsung readies Gaia AI accelerator for PCs — HP and Lenovo are reportedly validating the NPU
items.description: Samsung reportedly preps Gaia AI accelerator for client devices that is already being tested by HP and Lenovo.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 4 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Samsung is reportedly sampling its dedicated AI processor for next-generation AI PCs with leading PC makers, such as HP and Lenovo. The chip, codenamed Gaia, was developed by the company's System LSI business unit, and it is designed to offload AI-related workloads from the CPU and GPU, reports Chosun.Samsung's Gaia is designed to accelerate generative AI workloads on PCs and is made using the company's 4nm-class fabrication process. The chip, which is essentially a neural processing unit (NPU), is currently being evaluated by HP in the U.S. and Lenovo in China to verify its performance and evaluate whether it makes sense to integrate Gaia into their systems due in late 2027 or early 2028.The report does not detail how Gaia differs from NPUs that are integrated into AMD's Ryzen, Intel's Core, or Qualcomm's Snapdragon X processors as well as whether it can offer significant performance advantages. Meanwhile, the report implies that the NPU (or perhaps its derivatives based on the same architecture) could be used for Samsung's next-generation implementations of its processing-in-memory (PIM) technology.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Samsung's original PIM was designed to embed compute logic directly within the HBM memory array and reduce data movement between HBM memory modules and host processors. PIM was aimed to accelerate select workloads, but did not take off because AI and HPC GPUs became very efficient and were supported by mature ecosystems, unlike PIM. Perhaps if Samsung's upcoming Gaia NPU gains support from hardware makers and ecosystem partners, then this will give a boost to Samsung's next-generation PIM implementation as well. However, standalone NPUs and PIM are so fundamentally different that we can barely imagine that they can share a common architecture. Yet, PIM logic can be a subset of an NPU in terms of supported instructions and data formats and they can certainly share a common software framework.One of the interesting things to note about Gaia is that it was reportedly developed by Samsung's LSI division, the same business unit at the company that is responsible for Exynos processors, automotive solutions, connectivity chips, ISPs, DSPs, display drivers, and image sensors. Given the multi-faceted nature of Samsung's LSI unit, as well as its strategic importance for the company, Samsung must be pinning some hopes on Gaia. You may like China’s Huawei to enter South Korean AI chip market with new Atlas SuperPods, clusters pack 8,192 Ascend 950 accelerators per deployment Microsoft is reportedly testing Copilot+ AI features with discrete GPUs instead of NPUs Nvidia says AI cuts 10-month, eight-engineer GPU design task to overnight job We have contacted Samsung and asked for a comment about the report, but we yet have to hear back from the company.
